title: Quassia praecox
intro: Quassia praecox, also known as Quassia amara or Quassia sanguinea, is a species of plant in the family Simaroubaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which have been used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and intestinal parasites. Bioactive constituents of Quassia praecox include quassin, quassinoid glycosides, and simarin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Quassia praecox is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support digestive health and immune system function.
taxonomy: Quassia praecox is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Quassia, which contains about 10 species of tropical trees. The Rutaceae family is commonly known as the citrus or rue family, and it contains over 1,600 species of trees and shrubs. Quassia praecox is native to tropical Africa and is also found in other parts of the continent. The plant is known for its medicinal properties and is used in traditional medicine in some regions.
